task_completion.description: Set up the spreadsheet structure with spending categories and three credit cards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a LibreOffice Calc spreadsheet containing a list of spending categories (e.g., Groceries, Gas, Dining) and headers or labels for exactly three different credit cards.

task_completion.description: Enter cashback rates or reward multipliers for the three credit cards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for percentage rates, point multipliers, or reward values entered into the cells corresponding to the three credit cards and the spending categories.

task_completion.description: Calculate the cashback rewards for each card across the spending categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for calculated values (likely monetary amounts or total points) for each of the three credit cards in each spending category.

task_completion.description: Calculate the best (maximum) cashback amount for each spending category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dedicated column or row that displays the highest cashback value out of the three cards for each individual spending category.

task_completion.description: Recommend the optimal card choice for each category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a column or row that explicitly outputs the name of the best credit card to use for each spending category, indicating conditional logic was used to select the card with the highest reward.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to create the optimizer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the LibreOffice Calc application interface in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands generating a file.

integrity.description: The agent used spreadsheet formulas rather than hardcoding the final answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the formula bar in the screenshots. When a calculated cell or recommendation cell is selected, the formula bar should show a formula (e.g., MAX, IF, INDEX, MATCH) rather than static text or numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or markdown to simulate a spreadsheet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must clearly be a spreadsheet application with a grid, row numbers, and column letters, not a plain text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting up a spreadsheet for exactly three credit cards across multiple spending categories. The agent must calculate the cashback amounts, find the maximum cashback per category, and use conditional logic to output the name of the recommended card.
